channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Russia says Ukraine controls 15–17 per cent of Donetsk region
item.description: The Russian-installed head of ​Ukraine's Donetsk region, ‌Denis Pushilin, told Russian president Vladimir Putin ​at a ​meeting shown on state ⁠TV on Tuesday ​that Ukraine controls around ​15-17 per cent of the region. Russia claims the whole of ​the Donetsk region as part ​of Russia and says

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to meet EU's von der Leyen to push for restart of Druzhba oil flows
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Sunday he ‌would meet with EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday, as Slovakia and Hungary ramp up pressure on Ukraine to restart Russian oil flows, warning Slovakia could block a major EU ​loan to Kyiv. Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipelin

item.title: Explosion hits US embassy in Oslo, causing minor damage, police say
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion early on Sunday, causing minor damage but no injuries, Norwegian police said. A loud blast was heard near the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses told local media. "We've de

item.title: Plane carrying newly printed currency crashes, killing 15
item.description: Lockheed C-130 Hercules cargo plane carrying newly printed Bolivian banknotes crashed Friday near the capital, leaving at least 15 people dead, injuring others and damaging numerous vehicles, officials said. Defense Minister Marcelo Salinas said the aircraft skidded off the runway while landing at

item.title: Buses, trams and trains grind to a halt across Germany at start of 2-day strike
item.description: Buses, trams and trains across Germany ground to a halt early Friday as local transport workers heeded a call by the Verdi public sector union to stage a strike on Feb 27 and 28. The union is aiming to gain leverage in negotiations that cover working conditions, specifically working hours and shif

item.title: Cuba situation escalating, humanitarian needs must be solved: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said on Thursday that the situation in Cuba was heating up and called for restraint after a deadly incident with a Florida-registered speedboat off the coast of the Caribbean island. Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into Cuban waters aboard a speedboat

item.title: UK to enforce travel permit requirement on foreign visitors
item.description: Starting on Wednesday, visitors to Britain from 85 countries must obtain an electronic permit in advance of their trip or they will be barred from travelling, the UK interior ministry said. The Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) scheme requires all visitors who do not need a visa to enter Brita

item.title: Power drought tips Ukraine's economy into worst crisis since war's first year
item.description: Ukraine's economy is enduring its toughest period since the early months of Russia's invasion after sustained air strikes left its power system in tatters as the war enters a fifth year, forcing firms to cut output and shrinking state revenues. From steel mills to miners, cement makers and food pr

item.title: Spain's top court rejects father's bid to halt daughter's euthanasia
item.description: Spain's Constitutional Court has rejected an appeal by the father of a 25‑year‑old paraplegic woman who opposes her access to euthanasia, the court said in a statement on Friday. In 2021, Spain became the fourth European Union country to legalise euthanasia and assisted suicide for people with inc

item.title: Hungary considers cutting power, gas exports to Ukraine in Russian oil row
item.description: Hungary is considering cutting off power and gas exports to Ukraine unless Kyiv resumes Russian oil shipments to the country via the Druzhba pipeline, Prime Minister Viktor Orban's chief of staff told a briefing on Thursday. Hungary and Slovakia, which have the only remaining refineries in the Eur

item.title: Spain approves $8 billion in aid for storm-battered regions
item.description: The Spanish government approved financial aid worth around 7 billion euros ($8 billion) on Tuesday for people affected by the storms that battered the Extremadura and Andalusia regions in the past weeks. Spain and Portugal have been hit by several storms since the start of the year that have cause

item.title: Ukraine urges acceleration of peace talks, says only Trump can broker deal
item.description: Kyiv's foreign minister has said the Ukrainian and Russian leaders need to meet in person to hash out the hardest remaining issues in peace talks, and that only US President Donald Trump has the power to bring about an agreement. Ukraine wants to accelerate the efforts to end the four-year-old war

item.title: India seeks Hormuz passage as Tehran demands seized tankers back
item.description: Iran has asked India to release three tankers seized in February as part of talks ​seeking the safe passage of Indian‑flagged or India‑bound vessels out of the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, three sources ‌with knowledge of the matter told Reuters. Indian authorities seized the three Iran‑lin

item.title: EU debates how to curb energy costs as Iran war hits markets
item.description: European Union energy ministers on Monday debated ways to curb the impact of a surge in energy prices caused by the US-Israeli war on Iran. The bloc's heavy reliance on imported oil and gas means it is highly exposed to global price swings - leaving some officials and analysts doubtful that the EU

item.title: Greece not to engage in military operations at Hormuz Strait,: Govt official
item.description: Greece will not ‌engage in any military operations in the Strait of ​Hormuz, Greek government ​spokesman Pavlos Marinakis said on ⁠Monday. Greece would only ​participated in the EU's naval ​mission "Aspides" charged with protecting ships in the Red Sea, Marinakis ​told a press ​conference. U

item.title: Iran's Araqchi says Hormuz closed only to enemies
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said Iran had not requested a ceasefire and wanted to ensure that any end to the war with Israel and the U.S. was definitive. Speaking at a press conference, he also said the Strait of Hormuz was closed only to "enemies and those supporting their aggression".

item.title: Four US service members killed in plane crash over Iraq
Iran to allow India-flagged tankers through Hormuz: Indian source
FBI warns Iran may attack California with drones
US charter flight repatriating Americans from Middle East
Bill Clinton to lawmakers investigating Epstein: 'I saw nothing'
Takeaways from Trump's State of the Union address to Congress
US military preparing for potentially weeks-long Iran operations
Trump: Too busy to be a defendant but plenty of time to sue
White House uses USAID funds for budget director Vought's security, documents show
Ten dead after shooter opens fire at Canadian high school
US Vice-President Vance heads to Armenia, Azerbaijan to push peace, trade
NY governor signs law allowing medical aid in dying for terminally ill residents
